nodes.name: l0b_supply_chain
nodes.summary: Layer 0b analysis: supply chain auditing for Python (pip-audit), Node.js (npm audit), Go (govulncheck), and Rust (cargo-audit) dependencies, plus EOL version detection and typosquat checking.

nodes.name: l0c_dependencies
nodes.summary: Layer 0c analysis: checks for deprecated packages, outdated Python/Node dependencies via pip-audit and npm outdated, and license compliance scanning using pip-licenses.

nodes.name: l0d_behavioral
nodes.summary: Layer 0d analysis: behavioral metrics using git history to detect hotspots (high churn + complexity), knowledge drift (single-author files), and growing complexity via AST-based cyclomatic analysis.

nodes.name: l0e_iac
nodes.summary: Layer 0e analysis: Infrastructure-as-Code scanning for Dockerfiles (latest tags, root user), Kubernetes manifests (privileged containers, host networking), Terraform (public S3), and GitHub Actions workflows (pull_request_target), plus Checkov/KICS integration.

nodes.name: l0f_commit_risk
nodes.summary: Layer 0f analysis: commit risk assessment analyzing commit size, commit time patterns, message quality, tangled changes across directories, author patterns, and recent reverts.

nodes.name: l1_property
nodes.summary: Layer 1 analysis: property-based testing using Hypothesis to generate test inputs and detect edge cases, with support for custom test discovery.

nodes.name: l2_mutation
nodes.summary: Layer 2 analysis: mutation testing to assess test quality by making small code mutations and checking if existing tests catch them, with quick heuristic mode.

nodes.name: l3_invariants
nodes.summary: Layer 3 analysis: invariant checking using a JSON invariant database to validate static invariants like non-null checks, length constraints, and boundary conditions in function bodies.

nodes.name: l4_fuzz
nodes.summary: Layer 4 analysis: fuzz testing generation using Atheris to create fuzz harnesses for Python functions, with automatic harness generation and execution.

nodes.name: l5_policy
nodes.summary: Layer 5 analysis: policy enforcement using OPA (Open Policy Agent) for Rego policy evaluation, plus static policy checks for common code policy violations.

nodes.name: l6_symbolic
nodes.summary: Layer 6 analysis: symbolic execution integration for critical paths, leveraging external symbolic execution tools to find path-based vulnerabilities.

nodes.name: l7_simulation
nodes.summary: Layer 7 analysis: concurrency simulation and race condition detection for concurrent code paths, with pytest-based concurrency test execution.

nodes.name: l8_autofix
nodes.summary: Layer 8 analysis: automatic code fix generation with built-in fix patterns for eval usage, hardcoded passwords, Docker latest tags, and bare except clauses, plus Semgrep and Ruff integration for automated remediation.

nodes.name: learning
nodes.summary: Machine learning components: code embedding similarity via TF-IDF vectors, active learning for prioritizing findings for human review, and a lightweight GNN-inspired CPG scorer that extracts features from source code graphs to rank bug likelihood.

nodes.name: llm_verify
nodes.summary: LLM-powered bug verification: generates hypotheses about potential bugs using LLM, then verifies them by constructing and running test inputs, producing VerifiedBug results with CWE classification.

nodes.name: __init__
nodes.summary: Package init for the LLM module, re-exporting client and PRM scorer components.

nodes.name: client
nodes.summary: LLM client for local API communication: supports availability checking, prompt-based generation via HTTP POST to a local LLM endpoint, and structured finding review with JSON response parsing.

nodes.name: prm
nodes.summary: Process Reward Model scorer: evaluates LLM reasoning steps by analyzing logical connectives, step dependencies, claim consistency, and evidence grounding to produce step-level and overall quality scores.

nodes.name: __init__
nodes.summary: Package init for the LSP (Language Server Protocol) module.

nodes.name: server
nodes.summary: LSP server implementation providing real-time code analysis in editors: handles initialize, textDocument/didOpen/didChange/didSave notifications, with debounced analysis and diagnostic publishing for nullness, JS patterns, and code quality issues.

nodes.name: malicious_patterns
nodes.summary: Malicious code pattern detection: scans source files for backdoor indicators, obfuscation, data exfiltration, privilege escalation, reverse shells, and other supply-chain attack patterns using regex-based indicators.

nodes.name: maven_cve_db
nodes.summary: Maven CVE database and pom.xml scanner: parses Maven version ranges, maintains a cached CVE database updated from OSV, and scans pom.xml files for vulnerable dependencies including Spring Boot BOM version resolution.

nodes.name: metamorphic
nodes.summary: Metamorphic testing: classifies functions by type (sorting, hashing, string manipulation, math, compression, etc.), generates metamorphic test relations (determinism, idempotence, commutativity) using Hypothesis, and runs them to detect behavioral violations.

nodes.name: missing_patches
nodes.summary: Missing patch detection: scans codebases against a database of known vulnerable code patterns and their patched counterparts to identify unpatched CVEs in source code.

nodes.name: models
nodes.summary: Core data models: defines Severity, Category, BlastRadius, Decision, LayerID, Finding, AggregatedDecision, DiffHunk, PipelineResult, and LayerStats dataclasses that form the pipeline's type system and serialization layer.
